task: Identify cuBLAS HGEMM kernel selected at M=N=K=1536, dump SASS, structural-diff vs hexa N89 PS, list top-3 actionable micro-opts hexa is missing

cublas_kernel.name: void cutlass::Kernel2<cutlass_80_tensorop_s16816gemm_f16_64x64_32x6_nn_align8>(T1::Params)
cublas_kernel.sass_extraction_note: Kernel string appears in libcublasLt.so.12.9.2.10 strings table but the cubin is loaded via cuLibraryLoadData at runtime, not as a static ELF symbol. Only forwardCompat_256x128_64x3 variants are statically extractable. SASS used in diff is from a hand-written structural-proxy CUDA file matching the kernel-name spec, not byte-equal to cuBLAS.

HMMA.note: proxy has 4x more mma per K-step (matches cuBLAS structural pattern)

LDSM.note: proxy has 4x ldmatrix per K-step for full warp-tile

LDGSTS.note: hexa correctly uses cp.async; proxy fell back to LDG+STS due to inline-asm scaffold

1_pipeline_stages.root_cause: hexa K-loop emits cp.async.commit_group then immediately wait_all + __syncthreads, serializing load and compute. cuBLAS prologue-issues 5 cp.async groups then wait_group(N-2) inside steady-state, keeping 5-deep pipeline.

2_k_tile_size.root_cause: K=16 doubles absolute count of __syncthreads / BAR.SYNC / cp.async.commit_group boundaries vs K=32

3_cta_count.impact: +0.05-0.10 ratio if closed (large M); larger for small M
3_cta_count.root_cause: hexa 128x128 / 1024-thd tile = 4x fewer CTAs vs cuBLAS 64x64 / 128-thd tile. With 32 warps/CTA hexa fills SM fast but provides no inter-CTA latency overlap.

recommendations_summary.name: K-tile 16 -> 32 (double inner-K unroll)
recommendations_summary.scope: single-PR hexa edit (forces pipeline depth -> 4 due to shmem budget)

recommendations_summary.name: Tile 128x128 -> 64x64 + warps 32 -> 4
recommendations_summary.expected_ratio_gain: +0.05-0.10 at M=1536, larger for small M
recommendations_summary.scope: revert to N77-PP tile shape, retain N89 swizzle and primitives

not_recommended: wgmma.async / sm_90-native (out of scope -- cuBLAS uses sm_80 CUTLASS)
Persistent kernel / stream-K (structural change, not micro-op; separate PR after rec 1-3)
shmem XOR swizzle (hexa N89 already does this per N-stack pN heritage)
artifacts: hexa_n89_sass_1536.txt -- N89 SASS via ptxas -O3 --gpu-name sm_90
cublas_structural_proxy_64x64x32_s6.cu -- proxy source for SASS structure validation
instruction_histogram.csv -- per-opcode count hexa_n89 vs proxy
hgemm_probe.c -- minimal cublasGemmEx driver used to identify kernel via nsys
cublas_kernel_trace_first3.csv -- first row of nsys cuda_kern_exec_trace giving kernel name + launch geometry
diff_analysis.md -- structural diff + root-cause attribution of 0.557 ratio
recommendations.md -- top 3 actionable micro-opts with falsifiers
honest_limitations: True cuBLAS SASS for s16816gemm_f16_64x64_32x6_nn_align8 not byte-extractable -- kernel is loaded via cuLibraryLoadData from compressed cubin segments in libcublasLt.so. LD_PRELOAD interception of cuLibraryLoadData would dump it but is deferred (structural diff already exposes the dominant gaps).

Structural proxy compiles to a runnable kernel but has a misaligned-address fault at runtime in the epilogue STG indexing -- it is informative for the static SASS pattern only, not for byte-equal cuBLAS reproduction.
All measurements at M=N=K=1536 only. result.json from pS shows ratio varies 0.33 (M=384) to 0.56 (M=1536); recommendations are tuned to the M=1536 peak regime.
